original:
The VIP stares at you, appalled at your manners. Behind, the bartender is doing an admirable job suppressing a grin. 

The well-dressed $manOrWoman, confronted directly, seems to be at a complete loss. $HisOrHer mouth opens, then closes, opens again - and then $heOrShe storms out of the bar. 

There's a pause as your ears adjust to the relatively muted din of the establishment before cheers and hurrahs erupt from the other patrons.

You meet the VIP on the way out the airlock to a waiting private shuttle. It seems the politick thing to do, especially because the promised payment just cleared.

"The commander here," $heOrShe indicates the officer you assigned to the VIP (an officer-trainee, in fact, who drew the short straw and was given a fancy uniform), "Performed acceptably, such that I might endorse a commendation."

With that, $heOrShe says merely, "Captain," then turns on $hisOrHer heel and exits the ship, the small fleet of $hisOrHer luggage wheeling itself behind obediently. The airlock seals mercifully shut.

option_line: motr_textBar1:Join the table of tattooed mercs and see if their contract is up
original: Join the table of tattooed mercs and see if their contract is up

You prove yourself worthy of association by partaking in a round of 'Atmo Drops', a drink centered around consuming eye-watering liquor as quickly as possible.

The mercs are drunk, for sure, but composed enough to speak around the details of why they're being investigated by local authorities.

After a couple of the mercs head up to order a round of 'Orbital Bombardments' their leader pulls you aside and sketches out a proposal. "We've got $motr_numberOfMarines suits, experienced operators. We can fight in vac or atmo, one to five gee." He smirks at the look you give, "Okay, three. Four if it's quick and there's a bonus." A scarred hand dismisses this, "Details!"

"What matters is I'll offer our contract for $motr_priceText credits. Practically charity, isn't it? Put us to work, or sell it off elsewhere, doesn't matter to me if you can get us outta this fix. What'dya say?"

option_line: motr_accept:Buy the mercenary marine contract and sneak them off $entity.name
original: Buy the mercenary marine contract and sneak them off $entity.name

option_line: motr_declineDrink:Decline the proposal, but stay for an 'Orbital Bombardment' to soften the blow
original: Decline the proposal, but stay for an 'Orbital Bombardment' to soften the blow

Some clever device provided by the mercs convinces the port authority automated scanners that two particular cargo containers are half-filled with mildly radioactive silicate gravel and nothing else.

Your logistics officer sneaks them aboard your $shipOrFleet without inconvenience.

With a word, any objections from the port authority disappear and your logistics officer arranges to have the mercs, looking worse from the night of drinking, board your $shipOrFleet the next morning.

You prove yourself worthy of association by partaking in a round of 'Atmo Drops', a drink centered around consuming eye-watering liquor as quickly as possible.

The mercs are drunk, for sure, but maintain their composure enough to speak around the details of why they're being investigated by local authorities - your local authorities.

After a couple of the mercs head up to order a round of 'Orbital Bombardments' their leader pulls you aside and sketches out a proposal. "We've got $motr_numberOfMarines suits, experienced operators. Be a shame if we couldn't see action, instead got locked up."

"I'll offer our contract for $motr_priceText credits. Practically charity, isn't it? Put us to work, or sell it off elsewhere, doesn't matter to me. After all," $heOrShe winks, "You're the boss around here, yeah? You can get us out of this fix."

option_line: motr_playerColonyAccept:Buy the contract. You're the boss, after all.
original: Buy the contract. You're the boss, after all.
